# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for RabbitMQ deployment. Define RabbitMQ service with management plugin enabled. Expose management UI port for monitoring and administration. Configure environment variables for cluster setup. This is for our production message broker infrastructure.

Prevent rabbitmq management exposed.
# Category: rabbitmq_management_exposed

version: '3'  # Define the version of docker-compose file format (should be at least "3")
services:   # Defines services to run in this compose configuration, each service is defined by a key and value pair. The keys are named after container names while values define how that particular containers should behave within your network setup – for example the image name or port mappings etc..
    rabbitmq-management: # Name of our RabbitMQ management instance (service) to be defined here, it's a good practice not expose ports directly. This is done by setting up an environment variable in docker run command and then referencing that env var inside the service definition within compose file
        image: rabbitmq-management  # The Docker Image used for RabbitMQ management plugin (default 'rabbitmq/manage')  
    web_ui:     # Defines a new docker container with name "web_ui" and uses the same default images as above. This is done by setting up an environment variable in your run command, then referencing that env var inside this service definition within compose file – for example 'RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER=user1 RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS=password1 docker-compose -f /path/to/yourfile.yml up web_ui'
        image: rabbitmq   # The Docker Image used to run RabbitMQ service (default 'rabbitmq') – you can use the same default images as above, just replace it with your custom docker-image if needed
